entry.title: Loop control using SQLScript view, Data Flow script operator & Open SQL procedure in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Loop control is an effective programming concept that helps in dealing with repetitive execution of a logic over individual segments of an entire or sub dataset. While working with SQL, there are set-based operations and window functions that can help avoiding multiple execution of code blocks in loops. However, there can be use cases where we would end up using loops to achieve the desired outcome in certain deliverables. The objective of this blog post is to explore the possibilities of a less discussed topic so far - using conditional loops in Datasphere (based on a hypothetical use case).
I have taken a scenario where we have 2 measures, namely closing stock & inbound business units per article & store combination over a period of time ie. 16.02.2025 - 26.02.2025. Now I want to derive a calculated measure called interim new stock which should be derived as present day's inbound quantity added up with previous day's closing stock quantity for same article-site combination. (For the sake of simplification, I have not taken any outbound movement into consideration :)).
Let's say I have a table with below inbound & closing stock information in Datasphere within my space in Datasphere.

 

Calendar_DaySiteArticleUnitPurchase_UnitInbound_BUnClosing_Stock
2025-02-16S5673475896901EACA1720
2025-02-16S54610464863EACA1324
2025-02-16S54692105721EACA2112
2025-02-17S5673475896901EACA1123
2025-02-17S54610464863EACA1627
2025-02-17S54692105721EACA21219
2025-02-18S5673475896901EACA1219
2025-02-18S54610464863EACA1123
2025-02-18S54692105721EACA2721
2025-02-19S5673475896901EACA1312
2025-02-19S54610464863EACA1118
2025-02-19S54692105721EACA22436
2025-02-20S5673475896901EACA16040
2025-02-20S54610464863EACA1920
2025-02-20S54692105721EACA21240
2025-02-21S5673475896901EACA1134
2025-02-21S54610464863EACA1117
2025-02-21S54692105721EACA2341
2025-02-22S5673475896901EACA1620
2025-02-22S54610464863EACA1618
2025-02-22S54692105721EACA21032
2025-02-23S5673475896901EACA14860
2025-02-23S54610464863EACA11221
2025-02-23S54692105721EACA2327
2025-02-24S5673475896901EACA1248
2025-02-24S54610464863EACA1520
2025-02-24S54692105721EACA2720
2025-02-25S5673475896901EACA11043
2025-02-25S54610464863EACA1924
2025-02-25S54692105721EACA2321
2025-02-26S5673475896901EACA1432
2025-02-26S54610464863EACA11527
2025-02-26S54692105721EACA21230

I need a new target table which will have last 4 days' information from above table & a new calculated field called New_Interim_Stock which needs to be derived based on previous day's closing stock + present day's inbound quantity. For example, on 2025-02-26, for article 10464863 in site S546 the new interim stock would be 24+15 = 39. Below, I will try to demonstrate how we can do it applying loops in Datasphere through different approaches.

 

Approach #1 - using SQLScript view

SAP Datasphere supports the SQLScript syntax when the view editor is chosen as SQLScript (Table Function). Below I have attached the view definition script below with a conditional for loop which iterates over last 4 days of the dataset along with the calculated field New_Interim_Stock. I have created two different date variable pointers for handling current day (for inbound quantity) & previous day (for closing stock quantity) & a self join of the same dataset restricted by above date variables individually. The conditional for loop iterates over the dataset backwards starting from present day until it completes the calculation over the last four days.

 

 

declare control_date date;
declare today date default current_date;
declare date_var_inbound nvarchar(8);
declare date_var_stock nvarchar(8);
declare counter int default 1;
declare result_table table (
        "Calendar_Day" date,
        "Site" nvarchar(100),
        "Article" nvarchar(100),
        "Unit" nvarchar(100),
        "Purchase_Unit" nvarchar(100),
        "Inbound_BUn" int,
        "Closing_Stock" int,
        "New_Interim_Stock" int
        );
        
for counter in 1..4 do
    control_date := add_days(:today, -:counter);
    date_var_inbound := to_nvarchar(:control_date, 'YYYYMMDD');
    date_var_stock := to_nvarchar(add_days(:control_date, -1),'YYYYMMDD');

    insert into :result_table 
            select 
                inb."Calendar_Day",
                inb."Site",
                inb."Article",
                inb."Unit",
                inb."Purchase_Unit",
                inb."Inbound_BUn",
                inb."Closing_Stock",
                sto."Closing_Stock" + inb."Inbound_BUn" "New_Interim_Stock"
            from "inbound_stock_data" inb
            left join "inbound_stock_data" sto
            on  inb."Site" = sto."Site"
            and inb."Article" = sto."Article"
            where inb."Calendar_Day" = :date_var_inbound
            and   sto."Calendar_Day" = :date_var_stock;
end for;

return
select * from :result_table order by "Calendar_Day";

 

 

When the view is deployed, we can preview the data & validate output based on the data in source table.
Data preview of SQLScript view

Note : The output from the data previewing option of the view, which executes the conditional loop behind the scenes to get the output everytime we preview data. For larger datasets, an ideal solution would be to create a data flow with this view as a source & a target table with the output stricture in 'Append' data write mode. This will ensure that the output data is persisted & querying the same is free from the calculation latency.

 

Approach #2 - using Data Flow Script operator

In this approach, I created a dataflow with the source data table as a source, a target table for persisting the calculated result data & in between I have used the Script operator. The scripting language used here is Python.

I have attached the Python script below. The logic here is that I have sliced the dataset for the last 4 days backwards from maximum date available in the dataset ie. today. To begin with this time, I created with two Pandas dataframes. One of them is df_first, which is used to handle the oldest day of the sliced dataset. Because the sliced dataset has the first Calendar_Day as 23.02.2025, so the closing stocks for 22.02.2025 need to be fetched from the original dataset which is held in the df_first dataframe. On the other hand, df is sliced, grouped based on Article & Site & then fed to grouped_df dataframe. grouped_df dataframe is then consumed in the for loop alongside two date variable to point to the present day's data row & previous day's data row. The derivation of New_Interim_Stock is made within the if-else block, where the first day in the data slice is handled using the df_first dataframe based on article-site combination check. Finally the field New_Interim_Stock is updated within the df dataframe & a sorted result dataset is returned based on Calendar_Day.

 

 

def transform(data):

    df_first = pd.DataFrame(data)
    df_first['Calendar_Day'] = pd.to_datetime(df_first['Calendar_Day'], format='%Y-%m-%d')
    df_first['Calendar_Day'] = pd.to_datetime(df_first['Calendar_Day']).dt.date
    df = df_first.copy()

    today = df['Calendar_Day'].max()
    relevant_days = [today - pd.Timedelta(days = date_diff) for date_diff in range(0,4)]
    df = df[df['Calendar_Day'].isin(relevant_days)]

    for (article, site), group in df.groupby(['Article', 'Site']):
        grouped_df = group.sort_values(by = 'Calendar_Day', ascending = False)

        for date_diff in range(0,4):
            current_day = today - pd.Timedelta(days = date_diff)
            current_day_row = grouped_df[grouped_df['Calendar_Day'] == current_day]

            prev_day = current_day - pd.Timedelta(days = 1)
            prev_day_row = grouped_df[grouped_df['Calendar_Day'] == prev_day]
   
            if not prev_day_row.empty:
                new_stock = prev_day_row['Closing_Stock'].values[0] + current_day_row['Inbound_BUn'].values[0]
            else:
                prev_day_row_from_first = df_first[
                (df_first['Calendar_Day'] == prev_day) & 
                (df_first['Article'] == article) & 
                (df_first['Site'] == site)
                ]
                new_stock = prev_day_row_from_first['Closing_Stock'].values[0] + current_day_row['Inbound_BUn'].values[0]

            df.loc[(df['Article'] == article) & (df['Site'] == site) & (df['Calendar_Day'] == current_day), 'New_Interim_Stock'] = new_stock

    data = df.sort_values(by = 'Calendar_Day')

    return data

 

After the Data Flow is saved & deployed, execute the same & preview the persisted output data in the target table.

Output of Script operator

 

Approach #3 - using Open SQL procedures

The last option is basically utilising the conventional HANA studio experience. In Datasphere we can create procedures in your  open SQL schema if you have a DB user for the space in context or a DB usergroup user. Using the DB user credentials we can launch the database explorer or open the integrated HANA DB explorer console in VS Code. After that we can create procedures similar to HANA. The logic here basically follows the same structure that I used in the first approach with the SQLScript view.

Remember that in this case the output table has to be manually created within the same open SQL schema attached to the space where the procedure has write access. The open SQL schemas are access restricted, so for others to view the content of the output table, the table needs to be imported & deployed in the space by dragging into a view editor console for the first time use. Also for the same reason, if you have a local table in a space, the table might not be accessible for reading inside the procedure from your open SQL schema unless explicitly granted the select rights. In this case, the easiest alternative is to create a consumption enabled view on the local table & then it will be accessible by the open SQL schemas attached to the space.

Below is the script for the procedure. After the procedure is created, I simply called it in the console to update the output in the target table. For everyday usage, the procedure can be included in a task chain.

 

create procedure 
"PROC_FILL_NEW_INTERIM_STOCK"
language sqlscript as
begin

declare control_date date;
declare today date default current_date;
declare date_var_inbound nvarchar(8);
declare date_var_stock nvarchar(8);
declare counter int default 1;

for counter in 1..4 do
    control_date := add_days(:today, -:counter);
    date_var_inbound := to_nvarchar(:control_date, 'YYYYMMDD');
    date_var_stock := to_nvarchar(add_days(:control_date, -1),'YYYYMMDD');

    insert into table_proc_new_interim_stock
            select 
                inb."Calendar_Day",
                inb."Site",
                inb."Article",
                inb."Unit",
                inb."Purchase_Unit",
                inb."Inbound_BUn",
                inb."Closing_Stock",
                sto."Closing_Stock" + inb."Inbound_BUn" "New_Interim_Stock"
            from "Inbound_Stock_View" inb
            left join "Inbound_Stock_View" sto
            on  inb."Site" = sto."Site"
            and inb."Article" = sto."Article"
            where inb."Calendar_Day" = :date_var_inbound
            and   sto."Calendar_Day" = :date_var_stock;
end for;

end

 

Simply call & execute the procedure to insert the result dataset in target table.

 

call "PROC_FILL_NEW_INTERIM_STOCK";

 

To consume the table back in Datasphere build application, import & deploy the same & create a view on top of it.

As a blog-post wind up, I can add a final comment based on my experience.

  • When the source data is not persisted & accessed from remote source, the dynamic filter push down control of Script operator is not as strong as SQLScript view or DB Procedure, where you can control the push down by scripting in specific manner. But this observation can vary based upon the connection being used between Datasphere & the remote source system.

entry.title: In SAP Screen personas the text and text field are not getting alligned anymore. Personas 3.0 SP18
entry.content: Since last couple of days we have been observing a strange thing happening to the text fields(One line textfield) on the Personas. This was not the case earlier. We are in Personas 3.0 SP18.  When we reduce the height of the text box, the font of the text is getting reduced but not the box itself. See the screen shots. Can anyone help me on this? We need to reduce the size of the text boxes so that all of them fit on our screens.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - SFC WC POD View
entry.content:
Hi Community,
I received a production order from ERP.
After the order release from the DM, the SFC is checked in the Operation Activity POD (Default).
However, it is not viewed in Work Center POD (Default).
If you activate the Include Process Order through the POD designer, it will be viewed.
Order Category If you check, it is Production Order.
Why isn't it viewed in Default WC POD?

entry.title: Get Notified when New Excel Plugin Version
entry.content:
I would like to get notified via email when a new version of the Excel Add-in is released, instead of having to check the page regularly. How can I achieve this? Any help would be appreciated.

 

New to SAP world, find it little complicated to navigate. 

Version Information for the SAP Integrated Business Planning, Add-In for Microsoft Excel | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: What are the requirements for an OData service when creating an Analytical List Page application
entry.content: Facing the same issue with a OData V4 service with RAP. I added the mandatory annotation in the cds but still no luck :§

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Update Server restart
entry.content:
I have never heard about customers stating such things.

A Chrome update will not dictate a Personas upgrade. On the other hand, it is important to keep Personas itself updated regularly by implementing all released notes for it, especially if experiencing any problem. However, this has nothing to do with Chrome updates.

Similarly, a Personas upgrade itself will not cause "a server reboot". Which server is meant here?
It is recommended to perform SP upgrades when users who rely on flavors are not active, but this doesn't mean a requirement of bouncing the SAP system.

entry.title: When the datarg field in the integration center is changed, it is not pulling any information
entry.content: The datarg field that exists in the candidate and candidate profile in the integration center when changed, is not pulling any information nor when generating a report.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hi,

Firstly, Goto the Docker Website - sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 and in the info tab and please be clear about the password.

Secondly, Follow the Method:2 which I have mentioned to unlock SAP* user_id of each client and unlock any of the SAPGui SAP User_id and continue from there.

Thanks

Kushal

entry.title: Re: ADT access to ABAP Trial
entry.content:
Ajay,

Thank you for taking the time to confirm you are experiencing the same issue, greatly appreciated.

Hopefully we will get a response from SAP soon…

entry.title: Re: SAP Successfactors National ID Section to be mandator...
entry.content: Could you please check if the method in this blog can help? https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/useful-ways-to-use-variables-and-cardinality-rule-functions-in-onboarding/ba-p/13985465

entry.title: Re: ADT access to ABAP Trial
entry.content: To those who might be experiencing the issue can I kindly request you pen a quick post to confirm you are also experiencing the issue. This issue might be limited to a subset of users and not affecting all trial users, it will assist SAP in resolving the issue faster!

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA enablement
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 , here are responses to your questions:

1. "Customer has Workforce Analytics license" checkbox in Provisioning - the checkbox is required so you can see the WFA on Hana Data Factory menu.  Most of the time, if the customer has WFA license, this is already checked when someone provisioned the BizX instance as a prerequisite to install the WFA demo cube on the instance.  Probably that is why you rarely need to enable this setting.  However, I think there is a process change so WFA demo cube won't be installed as part of the BizX instance provisioning.  That's why you now have to check this when doing the WFA implementation.

I am not sure if there is a process running to check this setting against the license that customers have.  You need to check with support.

2. "Use WFA URL Set" in Provisioning - from what I understand this is normally setup as part of the Advanced Reporting setup when BizX instance is provisioned.  The advanced reporting is used for EC reporting as well so this is not dependent on WFA implementation.  My guess is since there is embedded story now to use for EC, customer may not setup Advanced Reporting and opt to use only embedded story so this is not always setup now.  As this setup is part of Advanced Reporting setup, so it is never included in WFA on HANA documentation.  However, we always mentioned that Advanced Reporting is a prerequisite to setup WFA on HANA, this means Advanced Reporting must work first before you can do WFA on HANA implementation.  If it has not been setup, then it is implementor responsibility to make sure this is setup and work.  Support should be able to help you to setup this.

3. Measure Views - support should be able to help upload the measure template.  If there is WFA demo cube installed before in the instance, it probably already has measure template setup.  The community is not the place to request for measure template because we do not always have access to the DC and instance to upload.

4. "IsAnalyticsInEc">True< - I've checked both starter and advanced template and this setting is setup as False.  I am not sure why Engineering said the template cause the issue.  We never had this issue with other implementations.  Most of the time this setting is already turned on for the instance and the setting to turn off is not even in the WFA on HANA Data Factory menu.  There is also a setting we need to turn off in provisioning related to this one: 

We need to run "Delete Cube" to remove the cube.

entry.title: Deprecation of Run Actual Settlement Application - no successor app
entry.content:
Run Actual Settlement Application is deprecated but no successor app is mentioned.

Please advise of the new app that will replace Run Actual Settlement.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Deprecation of Run Actual Settlement Application - no...
entry.content: Run Settlement - Actual (F4568) is the successor application.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @S0025138380 ,

It is possible to implement your scenario with alternative name element. But, I wonder what fields do you use to store preferred first name and preferred last name, as they are not standard field. Can I have the field id of them? I use business first name and business last name as example, you can define name format as following

Regarding details about name format definition, you may refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL/c18eb6c8febd4d7c924db8a257ce940d/799c9f0b46084896827c50613a392708.html

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA enablement
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 , here is response to your questions:

1. "Customer has Workforce Analytics license" checkbox in Provisioning - this is required to turn on so you can see the WFA on HANA Data Factory menu.  Usually this is already checked when someone provisioned the BizX instance and the customer has license for it.  It may also be turned on if customer want to have WFA demo cube installed in the instance.  But there is a change of process now that WFA demo cube is not always installed during BizX instance provisioned process so probably that's why you see some instances do not have this turn-on.

I am unsure if there is a background job that run to turn this off if customer has no license, you have to check with support.

2. "Use WFA URL Set" in Provisioning - this is a required setting for Advanced Reporting.  Again, usually setup when BizX instance get provisioned because EC also used Advanced Reporting for its reports.  However, since we now have embedded story, customer can opt to use story instead of Canvas report so it is possible this is not setup for certain customers.  As this is part of Advanced Reporting setup, it is not mentioned in WFA on HANA documentation.  But having Advanced Reporting is a prerequisite to implement WFA on HANA and this is mentioned in the WFA on HANA Welcome - Read me first documentation.  Support should be able to help with the setup.

3. Measure Views - Support should be able to upload the measure template.  If there was WFA demo cube installed in the instance before, there's a high chance that measure template has been uploaded before.  It is mentioned in the WFA on HANA Welcome - Read me first documentation to contact support to get measure template uploaded.  Please do not request for measure template upload in community as the people who monitor this forum may not have access to the target DC and instance to upload the measure template.  

If you get different response from support, most likely those who new in the support team or never handle WFA on HANA tickets before do not know the exact process.

4. "IsAnalyticsInEc">True< - I've checked both starter and advanced template and it is setup as False for this setting.  I am not sure why Engineering said it is caused by the template.  The menu to turn off this setting is not part of WFA on HANA Data Factory and there is a setting we need to turn off in the provisioning:

Need to run "Delete Cube" to remove the cube. 

Most of the time this setting already turned on for the instance.  It could be from some setup from Upgrade Center.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @x-eandrei ,

Yes, for Non-EC customers, there is no configuration to automatically update displayName. It is auto generated when create new employee, after that system will not auto populate it. Customer need to either import it, manage it on UI or integrate with master data system to maintain employee's displayName. 

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: Exploring GraphQL and REST for SAP Developers
entry.content:
Great blog

My understanding is GraphQL is really useful when data needs to be retrieved from multiple backends and consumed in single application.

Rather than creating multiple point to point connections, GraphQL take the complexity of multiple backends and prvides single facde layer

Regards

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @dianahromin ,

Yes, we will universal enable General Display Name for all customers in 1H 2025 release and it can't be disabled. 

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: SAP Standard Datasource Enhancement code select times.
entry.content:
Hi experts,

For example, the datasource 2LIS_03_BF had added a new field F1

and in the cmod , the code using "select" like "select ... into .... from EKKN for all entries in E_D_DATA.... ".

IF 2LIS_03_BF extracted 10000 records with full extraction one time ,  

my question is how many times the "select" occured ? 10000 times too ?

entry.title: #CoE#LAL Customize search criteria in Create Golden Tax Documents app
entry.content: In Create Golden Tax Documents app, can the Search Criteria  be customized?

entry.title: Re: Anyone implemented Inspector Qualification?
entry.content: The check should be in the Record Results functionality. The client wants certain MIC's to have results recorded by only specific users. For example, MIC_0, no Qualification, MIC_1 have IQ01 and MIC_2 have IQ02. All Inspectors can record results for MIC_0. For Inspector 1 (IQ01) can record also MIC_1. Inspector 2 (IC02) can record results for MIC_2 also, and Inspector 3 (IQ01 and IQ02) can record results for all 3 MIC's. They want the MIC's preferably in one Operation.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic assignment variant for Field-symbols not allowed
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi The code may be oversimplified in the original post and that may be the reason why you think it is coded without conditions. That's not the real case. In any case, since I haven't received any answers, I would have to assume that there isn't an alternative to this dynamic assign option.

entry.title: Oracle Client install and use in python operator in SAP Data Intelligence Cloud
entry.content:
We are trying to build custom operator to insert into Oracle table. But the below statement is looking for client library and failing. How can we point to oracle client library from python code in custom operator? We already prepared a vsolution for oracle client but this is not helping.

Code:

import oracledb
oracledb.init_oracle_client()

Error:

Cannot locate a 64-bit Oracle Client library: "libclntsh.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory"

entry.title: Re: Enabling ATC / CVA on BTP
entry.content:
Hi @S0021315792 Sweta Agarwal,

Thank you for your post. Yes, you can follow that blog. If you need further guidance or assistance please let me know.

Regards,

Hsin

entry.title: #CoE#LAL How to set journey entry header text detail?

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Change output file content in create Golden...
entry.content:
Hello Freya,

Please use IDGTCN_OB_OPR_DET_VALUE4TMPL for the output file content enhancement.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL How to set journey entry header text detail?
entry.content:
Hi Freya,

There's no standard feature to add vendor name into the Document Header text automatically. But vendor name can be found in the line item of AR side of JE, user can check the total payment of specific vendor by AR account. 

Enhancement required for have vendor name in header text.

BR,

Yong

entry.title: Re: Getting Appdoc error in MIGO
entry.content: I meet the same problem, I am also using IDES EHP8.

entry.title: Re: S4 identification(BUT0ID ) is getting deleted during C4C replication.
entry.content: Dear Team, I found the reason that C4C does not have that identification type configured. So whenever replication is happening identification getting deleted. to solve the issue, i excluded the entry in DRFF identification type filter 98699. once I maintained, it started working meaning deletion is not happening any more.

entry.title: Re: Service Call Scheduling via SDK
entry.content: Hi William, thanks for your response. This helped heaps!

entry.title: Re: Announcing SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 1ST March 2025
entry.content: Providing details about the event, such as session names, duration, and break times, would be really helpful.

entry.title: Re: In IFRS16, post the total value of the contract to the standard liability
entry.content: Elaborate "our client wants to standard the entry of the CONTRACT VALUE in a LIABILITY account, could you help us? "

entry.title: Re: How can we view the status of the database and backup...
entry.content: In the Technical Monitoring Cockpit, the database backup status and database version status are not available. Kindly suggest an alternative application. In the Technical Monitoring app, we can only analyze the workload of the database and application.

entry.title: Re: Facing Disconnection from SAP application to DB for e...
entry.content:
Hello Rajashekaram,
Seems issue related to your Database.
Please check the status of log backup related parameters and execute R3trans -d.
If the issue is resolved, please share the resolution.

entry.title: When testing PunchOut Catalogue, items are not being Added to Cart?
entry.content:
Hi all, first time as a supplier for SAP Ariba. We have created an L1 PunchOut catalogue (.cif) for our client and we are in the testing phase. During the testing of the catalogue, our site loads within the clients ERP and everything seems to display and function as per usual, however, when a product is selected and the user clicks the Add to Cart button, the Cart page appears empty, without the product that were just added?
We are using WooCommece to facilitate the PunchOut. Has anyone had a similar experience or can anyone shed some light on what the reason for this might be? I sincerely appreciate any assistance or suggestions. Thank you.

entry.title: while creating bp i got error supplier specific data were not saved due to error F2 007
entry.content:
upplier-specific data were not saved due to error F2 007 (see long text!)

Message no. CVI_BDT_ADAPTER051

Diagnosis

While saving the supplier-specific data of the business partner the following error occurred:

Account 79 already exists
F2 007

The business partner was created/changed, the corresponding supplier was not created/changed.

entry.title: Re: SAP Content Network (Transfer between two clients / D...
entry.content:
1. yes, it's possible. 

2. Almost all data, meta data, master data and fact data can all be transported, depends on how you export. 

3. No, it's same as story.

entry.title: Re: Exploring GraphQL and REST for SAP Developers
entry.content:
Great Blog and great comment section discussion.
Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Datasource Enhancement code select times.
entry.content: Select statement hits database once per one package. For example total records extracted are 100000, and package size is 10000 then in total select is going to run 10 times.

entry.title: Re: Customer Hierarchy In Condition Contract
entry.content:
Hello Victor,

Thanks for your reply. Yes I do have the following setting. My calculation lies within the Customer Hierarchy validity period

Do you suggest any particular structure to check in the Business volume profile?

I have created a Pricing table including Customer hierarchy (HIENR), but at item level in settlement document pricing, Customer Hierarchy is not recognized. Appreciate your inputs

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA New O/P management replace idocs with APIs
entry.content:
Hi,

We have our current solution based on idocs, where in we send the billing documents from SAP to third party system. Biztalk is the middleware, which transforms the idoc details and sends it in XML format.

We are exploring the options of replacing the idocs with API and check the feasibility of using new S4 HANA new output management.

Has anyone explored this option ? Kindly share your thoughts.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Distributing SAP Enable Now Content
entry.content: Hi Dirk, can you help me with a link where this licensing consumption policy is mentioned? I need to share it to one of my user..

entry.title: Hi,Can anyone help me in understanding how the cost is calculated in ordering company code?
SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 6: SAP Analytics Cloud & SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
This sixth post in my series gives an overview of latest user experience (UX) innovations in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The highlights for SAP Analytics Cloud are the support for the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, giving users an experience consistent with other products; the Compass for business users to easily do Monte-Carlo simulations; Video Data Stories, and enhancements to the just ask natural language query capability. For SAP BTP, have a look at the new UX for the SAP Integration Suite, SAP BTP Kyma runtime, and SAP Build Apps.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

In addition to full support for the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, the Q1/2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud released on February 26th brings some great new experiences for users. I’ll focus here on these three:

  • The new Compass for easy Monte Carlo simulations by business users.
  • Video Data Stories, enabling business users to easily create videos to present their stories.
  • Just Ask enhancements.

Support for SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

SAP Analytics Cloud now also supports the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon – not only for embedded uses cases as before, but now stand-alone too. As a result, users of our cloud applications can have a consistent experience when switching to SAP Analytics Cloud. Figure 1 shows an example.

Figure 1: SAP Analytics Cloud with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: An example screenshot showing what chart, table, input control and widgets look like with the Horizon visual theme.

SAP Analytics Cloud Compass for Monte Carlo Simulation

Non-technical users can now easily perform real-time multivariant analysis without prior IT setup or advanced statistic skills. You can trigger a new compass simulation via the toolbar or directly from a dashboard. If things are not going as planned, for example the gross revenue in the current quarter, you can trigger a simulation which will automatically detect the drivers and display probability distributions for gross revenue.

You can choose between low, medium or high precision, which uses 1,000, 10,000 or 100,000 iterations respectively. As you can see in Figure 2, this is visualized interactively, showing the probability for the current value. This is visualized by showing how likely the operating income will be exceeded (23% in the example) and how likely it will not be achieved (77%). At the bottom, you can see ranges for the pessimistic, realistic and optimistic cases (lower 5%, middle 90% and upper 5%). Since it is interactive, you can change the planned value and directly see how likely you are to exceed or not achieve it – as you can see in Figure 2.

Figure 2: SAP Analytics Cloud Compass for Monte Carlo Simulation by business users. ALT Text: This short embedded GIF video shows a screen with a header “Target: Operating Income / Amount GC – Million USD”. Below that, a chart showing a probability distribution for Operating Income, looking similar to a normal distribution. It has a vertical line at 87.51, indicating that the probability of less income is 5% (labelled “Pessimistic Case”, and a vertical line at 175.20, indicating the probability of more income is 5% (labelled “Optimistic Case”). The 90% band in between is labelled “Realistic Case”, with a slider at 151.67 (shown in a box above the slider), with 77% shown on the left and 23% on the right (the probability of income being lower or higher). The user moves the slider to the left with the mouse to the value 106.10, and the two values to the left and right change to 16% and 84%. Then the user types in a value of 100, resulting in 12% and 88%.

Find out more about this, and watch it in action in this video:

  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 2 on Compass) (2:35 min.)

Video Data Stories

Another great user experience innovation with the latest release is the ability for business users to directly create short videos to present their data stories out of SAP Analytics Cloud – Figure 3 shows an example.

Figure 3: Part of a Video Data Story created from SAP Analytics Cloud. ALT Text: A short video with animated text and charts in a mobile phone vertical format. It starts with the title “Sep 2024, results”, followed by “Gross Margin”. Then the text “$8.66 million” glides in, followed by “delta 2024 Aug +37.45%”. Next, these disappear and “Gross Margin per Location” appears, above a horizontal bar chart with three bars for California ($6.78 M), Nevada ($0,52 M) and Oregon ($1.36 M). This chart then morphs into a new horizontal bar chart with five bars for Los Angeles ($2.45 M), Oakland ($1.22 M), San Diego ($1.62 M), San Francisco ($0.29 M), and San Jose ($1.21 M).

It is really easy to create video data stories: you can select which data you want to show, how you want to display it (Figure 4), and select a background image and background music. We provide a selection of images and music out of the box for you to choose from.

Figure 4: Creating Video Data Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud. ALT Text: The image shows part of a screenshot showing an SAP Analytics Cloud page with bar charts and a pie chart, partly covered by a section below which shows five views of the video data story, containing (from the left) the title, a horizontal bar chart with three bars, a pie chart, a line chart, and a KPI as a number.

Find out more in this blog post, these videos and the documentation:

  • Video Data Story, a brand-new way of story telling in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Video: Video Data Story – Sample Analysis Report (33 seconds).
  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 3 on Video Data Stories) (1:30 min.)
  • What’s New Viewer – SAP Analytics Cloud.

Just Ask Enhancements

The popular just ask feature enables you to use natural language to query SAP Analytics Cloud. It was introduced with this blog post:

  • Initial Release of SAP Analytics Cloud Just Ask.

With the Q1/2025 release, we improve just ask by supporting currency conversion in acquired data models. We also allow users to limit the number of data points in bar charts, as well as leveraging SAP HANA Cloud vector engine.

Currency conversion means that, if you have data with multiple currencies, you can change the currency used to summarize all the values using natural language. Also, you can choose to show all the results in the respective local currency, so that for example the mouse-over shows the details and the currency for that country, as shown in Figure 5, where the user has asked “sales in local currency actual compared to forecast”.

Figure 5: SAP Analytics Cloud just ask now supports currency conversion – here you see a visualization in local currency per country. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with Just Ask at the top left, with a text entry field containing “sales in local currency actual compared to forecast”. Below that, a horizontal bar chart showing “Top 10 Sales in Local Currency per Country”, with countries Denmark, Belgium, Italy, Sweden, France etc. Wherever the actual is higher than forecast the difference is marked with a green bar, where it is lower with a red bar. The user has placed the mouse over the green bar for Sweden, showing a popover containing the text “Sales in Local Currency, SEK909.2 k, Country Sweden, in green: Actual – Forecast SEK168.7K”.

Watch it in action here:

  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 4 on just ask) (1:34 min.)

Further Innovations and Outlook on Joule Analytical Pattern

You can see the above three innovations, plus “Seamless Planning” and “Job Monitor – Multi-Action support” in this video:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025.

As I mentioned in Part 1 of this series, we plan to make the new analytical pattern for Joule available in Q2/2025, which will be powered by this latest release of SAP Analytics Cloud (as usual for forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change). This means users of products that support Joule, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP SuccessFactors, will be able to use Joule to query SAP Analytics Cloud and get analytical insights directly within Joule (the Joule Analytical Pattern). It will be powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform

SAP Integration Suite

The SAP Integration Suite now also supports the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, as you can see below in Figure 6.

Figure 6: SAP Integration Suite with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: The image shows three overlapping screenshots: at the left, back, “Employee Service” with buttons for triggering GET, POST, GET, PATCH URLs. Next, in the middle, the Capabilities page showing three rows of four columns of cards such as “Build Integration Scenarios”, “Manage APIs”, “Manage Business Events”, each with two to three lines description. On the right a page showing details of “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration with Workday”. The “Artifacts” tab has been selected, showing a list of artifacts with columns Name, Type, Version and Actions. The first entry has a name “Integrate Workday employee information”, the second one “Replicate Cost Centers from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Workday”.

SAP BTP Cockpit

Customers can now get an easy to understand overview of their costs and usage of SAP Business Technology Platform with the new costs and usage application (Figure 7).

Figure 7: SAP BTP Cockpit – Costs and Usage. ALT Text: The image shows an example screenshot. The header reads “Global Account: Automotive Rentals, Inc. – Costs and Usage”, below that a small card “Global Account Info” with Account ID, Account Type “Customer”, Contract Currency “USD” and a wide card “Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement” with a chart showing Cloud Credit Usage, a KPI value for the Monthly Cloud Credit Usage and a vertical bar chart for Monthly Cloud Credit Usage Trend. Below that the majority of the screen shows the Billing tab, with the text “This view displays the monthly billable service charges for your global account”. Below that a flexible column layout with a list of Service Plans on the left, with “Business Application Studio – standard-edition” selected, and on the right a hierarchical table showing Directories and Subaccounts. At the bottom a filled line chart showing the cumulative list prices per month over the last 12 months.

SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime

Kyma now supports modules, for adding functionalities to your cluster. To manage these, the Kyma dashboard has been redesigned, with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, as you can see in Figure 8.

Figure 8: Kyma dashboard with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, now supporting modules. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with the “Cluster Overview” selected in the left side navigation bar. The screen has a large header card “Introducing Modules” with buttons for “Add Modules” and “Learn More”. Below that a section “Resource Details” containing one card, followed by a section “Monitoring and Health” containing further cards (“Memory Usage”, “CPU Usage”). A further smaller screenshot covers the bottom right of the other one, showing a Modules column in the middle, and a wider “Add Modules” panel on the right, containing four cards “SAP BTP Operator”, “Keda”, “Serverless”, “Telemetry”.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps enable business users to define their own simple applications without having to go to their IT teams to find developers. For these users, an intuitive user experience is paramount, and hence we continuously look to see where we can make it even better. One aspect of intuitiveness is providing a consistent experience across various products – hence SAP Build Apps also runs with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon.

Figure 9: SAP Build Apps with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with a heading “Choose Configuration to Build” above six square cards “Android (AAB)”, “Android (APK)”, “iOS (Ad hoc)”, “Web (MTAR)”, “Web (ZIP)”, “workzone build”. Below that a table “Build History” showing two lines for “iOS (Ad hoc)”.

Here are some of the recent improvements:

  • You can create a project version directly now by releasing the project
  • Discover, integrate, and use any preconfigured action that is published in the SAP Build Library: you can now easily discover published Actions in SAP Build Apps via the SAP Build Library and install them into your Build Apps project, consume and trigger them as part of your application’s business logic via the Trigger Action flow function.
  • In the History tab, you can now see all previously saved states of the app. Using the Restore button, you can restore the application to the respective saved state.
  • You can duplicate pages in the page preview header.
  • You can use the updated Build service, which comes with a better look and feel, and an improved user experience:
    • Build and Deploy Web Applications.
  • The details matter with UX! To improve the user experience, we have updated the navigation menu in the global toolbar (Figure 10). App developers now have access to the Variables directly from the menu. The previous UI Canvas and Data tabs are now renamed respectively to User Interface and Integrations. To save space, the less frequently used Theme, Navigation, Authentication and Help options are now grouped under the App Settings drop-down menu. In the upper right corner, you can access the Preview button. Next to it is the Publish button, which enables you either to Create a Release Version or to Build and Deploy.

Figure 10: The new navigation menu in the SAP Build Apps global toolbar. ALT Text: A screenshot showing the header of SAP Build Apps, which contains tabs “User Interface”, “Variables”, “Integrations”, and “App Settings” with a drop-down menu with entries “Navigation”, “Authentication”, “Theme”, and “Help”.

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this sixth part, covering SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.

I will post the final part seven next week, covering latest innovations for UI developers, for both web and mobile.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps
entry.content: Hi @Saikat7 Do you mean you want to train your own model with the data from SAP S/4HANA? The question is unclear to me as custom prompt means something different, see here https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/ai-builder/create-a-custom-prompt

entry.title: requirement is to develop entire smart form in Hindi language
entry.content:
requirement is to develop entire smart form in Hindi language. Can anyone share step by step process? It would be really helpful. Dynamic text will be in English Language Only. Fixed Template needs to be develop in Hindi. Do I need to create a different smart style as well for Hindi Text? Please Guide. 

Thanks & Regards,

Darshana Jagtap.

entry.title: Re: Set up your SAP S/4HANA system for EAM Inspection Checklists
entry.content:
In Inspection Check list scenario, Have set up a master data with same class characteristic (ACMC) in Object and inspection plan (ACMC)and checklist type(AMC_ELEC & AMC_MEC) in Task list operation & inspection plan classification (AMC_ELEC & AMC_MEC) are matching. in first time order creation the Inspection lots generated automatically (CLGE). 

after i have created other master data set up with same classification task list, inspection plan, object and material with same classification earlier used (ACMC and AMC_ELEC, AMC_MEC),

A)  if i release the order inspection lots are not generating, status saying CLNO. even though the Classification Characteristics are match with object and inspection plan

Ajit

entry.title: Inspection Specifications from Configuration Checkbox is missing
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue in the Production client where the "Inspection Specifications from Configuration" checkbox is missing in the Quality Management view of the Material Master for a KMAT (Configurable Material). This checkbox is visible and works as expected in the TEST client, but it is not displayed in the Production client.

We kindly need assistance to resolve this issue as it is impacting our go-live which is on 3rd February. Please provide guidance on:

Why the "Inspection Specifications from Configuration" checkbox is missing in the Production client.

Any configuration or settings that might have been overlooked.

Steps to enable the checkbox in the Production client.

Thank you very much!

Test client

 Production Client

 



Best Regards,
Kaushika

entry.title: Re: How to populate entity with typed values from externa...
entry.content:
Assuming you declared your type and entity like the below:

type Amount {
    value    : Decimal(10, 2);
    currency : String(3);
};

entity Expenses {
    amount : Amount;
};

Then the resulting data structure will be:

export function _ExpensAspect<TBase extends new (...args: any[]) => object>(Base: TBase) {
  return class Expens extends Base {
    declare amount_value?: number | null
    declare amount_currency?: string | null
    static readonly kind: 'entity' | 'type' | 'aspect' = 'entity';
    declare static readonly keys: __.KeysOf<Expens>;
    declare static readonly elements: __.ElementsOf<Expens>;
    declare static readonly actions: globalThis.Record<never, never>;
  };
}

Resulting in a table like this:

CREATE TABLE ns_Expenses (
  amount_value DECIMAL(10, 2),
  amount_currency NVARCHAR(3)
);

So there is no column 'amount'.

To resolve this, you should 'flatten' your data before doing an insert into the database, which you can either do manually (amount_value = amount.value), or I typically use a function for that:

export function flattenObject(object: Record<string, any> | null, parent?: string) {
    const flattened: Record<string, any> = {}
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(object ?? {})) {
        if (typeof v === 'object' && !Array.isArray(v) && v !== null) {
            Object.assign(flattened, flattenObject(v, parent ? `${parent}_${k}` : k))
        } else {
            flattened[parent ? `${parent}_${k}` : k] = v
        }
    }
    return flattened
}

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @HarveyXu ,

has there been talks between the SAP SuccessFactors and SAP HCM team with regards to the SAP HCM add-on and the Talent Hybrid mode for SuccessFactors to get have the SAP HCM add-on enhanced with the displayName field? Otherwise I would expect that for SuccessFactors non-EC customers there is a functionality provided to have the display name updated automatically if one of the name fields (first name, last name, ...) is changing. Manual maintenance via import or UI is not an efficient solution.

Thanks and regards
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Custom routine for pricing procedure not performing c...
entry.content:
Just return the 'xkwert=lv_discount' in your routine. You may check other routines('Alternative Calculation of Condition Amount' ) that are delivered by SAP in V/08 as a kind of example.

Instead of using ''Alternative Calculation of Condition Amount'' routine, for your case, it`s better to have 'Requirement', then only return TRUE/FALSE in there. But the prerequisite is first to define a discount VK12.

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion Announcements & Context Data
entry.content:
Hi Marie,

Yes it is possible to modify the context on individual Guided Tour Steps is the corresponding screen changes.  To do this:

1. Open SAP Companion in editing mode for your Guided Tours and then open the Guided Tour that requires modification
2. Click on the Guided Tour Step to be modified and select Edit
3. Click on the Element tab
4. Click the button that contains a circle with an arrow head at the end of the Recorded Screen ID field
5. Save the changes and publish if it is not ready for end users

To create What's New content:
1. Open SAP Companion and navigate to the What's New tab
2. Click the + and select the type of content to be created.  Because you start the creation in the What's New tab, you are creating What's New content

If there is no What's New tab for your current screen:
1. Open SAP Companion in editing mode
2. Click the + and select Add What's New
3. Click the + again and start and create your content

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: What are the advantages of the Sinon.JS framework?
entry.content:
You're right that A, B, and C are all advantages of Sinon.JS. The question is poorly worded because it implies only two are correct when in fact three are.

The most likely scenario is that the question is flawed. If forced to choose only two, options A and C would be the strongest choices because they represent core fundamental features. While B builds on C, A is the broadest and most defining capability of Sinon.JS. Option D, while possible, isn't a feature of Sinon itself; Sinon makes TDD easier, but doesn't enable it in a way that's uniquely defining.

Therefore, while the question is flawed, selecting A and C as the "best" two answers is the most defensible approach given the options.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Message Channel Bindings
entry.content:
Dear Colleague,

please read the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/team-fpm-abap-push-channel-for-fpm-events-enhancing-the-user-experience/ba-p/13575332, respectively implement SAP Note 3406803.

You can address this problem with the importing parameter  IV_DELIVERY_TYPE when sending an FPM Event.

Thank you & Best regards,

Simon

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Hi All,Can we convert from Classical GL to New GL during the S4Hana conversion.
entry.content:
Hi All,

We upgraded to the Hana Database two years ago, and we currently have the EHP8 for SAP ERP 6.0. Although we are currently using the classical GL and have not yet upgraded to the new GL, my company plans to move to a full S4 Hana conversion.

Can we convert from Classical GL to New GL during the S4Hana conversion, or should we convert to New GL first and then to S4Hana?

If answer is Possible to convert directly Classic GL to  S4hana what are the Pros and Cons.

Really Help your suggestions.

Thanks,

Lakshman

2> Activate Vas log and check log

3> Debug the ClassSCWM/CL_VAS_MODEL method FILL_TO_POST with your ABAP...

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Sort Key in Vendor Master
entry.content:
The Sort Key of Vendor is available in the table: LFB1 (Known as Vendor Master (Company Code)).
The field is: ZUAWA

LFB1-ZUAWA

entry.title: Re: Manage Posting Periods - Cost Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:

Please set posting periods manually or schedule jobs one by one.

Could you pls let us know how we can Schedule recurrence to  All Account types ( S.M.K,D A ) for Close and Open Posting every month.

 

Thank 

JP

entry.title: Re: Unlock the Power of SAC Custom Widgets: Personalize Your Excel Exports
entry.content:

Really good article. Thank you for sharing. Just wondering if I have 20 columns and 10 rows in a table in a story, can I use some script to break it down to 4 (5 columns * 10 rows) tables listed vertically one by one via downloading to PDF?

Or if it is possible to break it down to 4 tables in the story automatically if originally I have only 1?

Kind regards,
Fiona.

entry.title: Re: MS Dynamics to SAP BW Integration

entry.title: Group Reporting Balance Validation - errors in new functionality provided in 202502 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi, I have two issues with the Group Reporting New Balance validation setup testing in the starter system

  1. Consunits and Consgroup aren't available in the selection dropdown in the Assign Rule Group for Valance Validation App for each of the tasks
  2. The consolidation monitor is still looking for the old task (category 70) after the assignment of the new task S2980 into the consolidation monitor task group Z20.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Balance Validation - errors in new fu...
entry.content:
There are several steps when you want to configure this balance validation.

First,  You need to activated the feature Toggle for FE of the Balance Validation (FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION) in the configuration activity Activate Features based on help Balance Validation

Second, display, change, and create validation rules and rule groups and activate them for validation in app  Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups
 
Thirdly,  Use this app Assign Rule Groups for Balance Validation to assign rule groups to consolidation units and consolidation groups (depending on the valid execution category setting made in the Define Balance Validation Tasks configuration activity), special version, and period. The assignment is version-dependent. 
Finally, you can use these validation in Data/Consolidation monitor.

entry.title: Re: How to populate entity with typed values from externa...
entry.content: that explains the error message I get. I am just not sure where to flatten the object, as my on.READ handler was not reached. But the idea is understood.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2025 公有云深度赋能系列合集
entry.content:
从产品专家到产品专家

在云ERP产品成功团队，我们始终致力于使我们的客户和合作伙伴充分利用SAP S/4HANA 公有云版的全面解决方案，并从他们的投资中获得最大价值。

通过我们的“SAP S/4HANA 公有云版深度解读”计划，我们提供一系列深入的专题会，分享产品专家的知识，详细解释SAP S/4HANA 公有云版的新功能和特性以及其为业务带来的创新价值。

 

2025年第一季度课程安排

S/4HANA产品深度赋能系列 - 公司间对账和集团报告的集成
  • 点击此处注册
  • 2025年1月10日

 

观看2024年录播

 

如何报名参加深度解读系列课程？

如果想报名参加在线课程，您只需要点击上方链接注册，并届时访问。

 

想要我们的下一次课程更符合您的需求？

请在下方留言告知您希望学习的主题或产品场景，与专家交流并提问。关注PSCC_CCC_CN 标签，获取我们最新的博客文章。

 

观看我们为 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502更新发布系列的博客！

从1月21日到1月24日，我们举办了一系列17篇精彩的博客，展示了 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502版本发布的激动人心的创新，涵盖众多端到端的话题：制造、研发工程（产品设计与开发）、供应链管理和质量、财务、技术、用户体验、测试、解决方案订单、RASD工具、AI人工智能与技术、寻源和采购话题、销售话题。您可以按需阅读整个系列或选择最相关的话题进行了解——随时随地，不容错过。

SAP ERP公有云 - 汇总更新：阅读博客

entry.title: Re: Eclipse(HANA Development): Error when searching for a...
entry.content: Hi lkysiak, I'm glad that you have also fixed your problem. I only kept one plugin—the old one: org.apache.commons.logging_1.2.0.v20180409-1502.jar. When I say that I renamed the more recent one, I mean that I changed its extension so that Eclipse wouldn't find it: org.apache.commons.commons-logging_1.3.4.jar.old

entry.title: Re: On Integration Suite page, left menu items are not vi...
entry.content:
Hi @Gilseok 

The provisioning is completed and that's why you are able to see the home screen. To do more, you must assign roles to your user. Please follow the instructions mentioned here in the official product documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configuring-user-access 

Hope this helps!

Regards,
Karuna

entry.title: Re: how to pass values to messages dynamically
entry.content: Na bhai sabu bujhiheigala au..

entry.title: Re: 0FI_AR_9 is Not Whitelisted. What is the Alternate?
entry.content:
Hi Shilpi,

Can you firstly have a look the note below: https://me.sap.com/notes/3484814

It tells that 0fi_AR_8/9 were replaced by SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR) and are not available in S/4HANA. 0FSCM_CM_0 ~ 0FSCM_CM_52 are supported in S/4HANA.

So far, I didn't see any standard credit management CDS view can be used for data extraction (need have annotation extraction.enable = true).

Regards,
Maggie

entry.title: Re: MISSING MANDATE REFERENCE
entry.content:
Hello,

Here's what the expert replied:

Based on the initial analysis, I think the issue lies with the document's posting entry in Financing not being properly integrated well with the SEPA mandate.

Please provide more detailed information regarding which entry that customers use to post accounting document, UCOI or Post outing Invoices or others?

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Custom routine for pricing procedure not performing c...
entry.content: @ArcherZhang Would I not need to make use of the komk tables as well? Because I have tried that before but was not quite working out

entry.title: Conversion from planned orders to production order - Related routing is not picked
entry.content:
Hello,

we have a scenario where we convert planned orders with explosion date in the past to production orders.

In OPL8 ‘BOM Explosion’ flag is set to ‘2’. This works fine as far as updating the BOM and Routing explosion dates with actual dates are concerned. The modified BOM is also copied to the production order but the routing stays old on operations level even though the group and group counter in the Master data tab in created production order are shown correctly. 

If we double click the routing group the the production order master data tab, it shows the correct modified routing and group counter but it doesn't reflect in the new order's operations; they still show the old routing operations.

Sure we can start CO02 and then re-read the master data which refreshes the routing and works but that doesn't fit with our process.

Any ideas are highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Time Variance not working for period comparison in SA...

Hi,

I tried to appply thE solution given by you, but when I select previous time interval it gives me previous month data. For exmple,  it given me Jan 2025 month i.e. only for one month back and not the option like you have previous month/period. how did you manage to see that option ?

Thanks for reply and support on this topic

entry.title: Re: Issue with Kit to Stock- Post Kit not visible
entry.content: it is hard to say without debugger, but could you check your packaging specification, do you have external step defined there?

entry.title: Re: How to populate entity with typed values from externa...
entry.content: depends a bit on your overall code, but I noticed your query "expenses" and on handler "Expenses" are not the same case-wise. Could be due to that.

entry.title: How to do a post with fm or bapi like in vl02n?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
How to do a post like in vl02.
I tried this, but it's only change a picking quantity.

 

 

DATA:
        is_dlv_data_control TYPE  bapidlvbuffercontrol,
        it_delivery_header  TYPE TABLE OF  bapidlvhdr,
        it_delivery_item TYPE TABLE OF bapidlvitem,
        r_vbeln TYPE RANGE OF vbeln,
        s_vbeln LIKE LINE OF r_vbeln,
        vbpok_ls TYPE vbpok,
        kostk TYPE vbuk-kostk,
        vbpok_lt TYPE TABLE OF vbpok,
        vbkok_ls TYPE vbkok,
        return2 TYPE bapiret2,                        "#EC NEEDED
        prot_lt TYPE TABLE OF prott.

  DATA:
      ef_error_any_0 TYPE xfeld,                                    "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_item_deletion_0 TYPE  xfeld,                      "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_pod_update_0 TYPE  xfeld,                         "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_interface_0 TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_goods_issue_0 TYPE  xfeld,                        "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_in_final_check_0 TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_partner_update TYPE  xfeld,                          "#EC NEEDED
      ef_error_sernr_update TYPE  xfeld .                         "#EC NEEDED

  FIELD-SYMBOLS:
                 <fs_delivery_item> LIKE LINE OF it_delivery_item.

  CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EVVBLKE'
    EXPORTING
      vbeln          = vbeln
    EXCEPTIONS
      foreign_lock   = 2
      system_failure = 3.

    if sy-subrc = 1.

    elseif sy-subrc = 2.

    elseif sy-subrc = 3.

    endif.

*Проверяем статус общее комплектование по поставке укомплектована = 'C'
  SELECT SINGLE kostk INTO kostk FROM vbuk WHERE kostk <> 'C' AND vbeln = vbeln.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

  is_dlv_data_control-item = 'X'.

  s_vbeln-low = vbeln.
  s_vbeln-option = 'EQ'.
  s_vbeln-sign = 'I'.
  APPEND s_vbeln TO r_vbeln.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_DELIVERY_GETLIST'
    EXPORTING
      is_dlv_data_control = is_dlv_data_control
    TABLES
      it_vbeln            = r_vbeln
      et_delivery_header  = it_delivery_header
      et_delivery_item    = it_delivery_item.

  LOOP AT it_delivery_item ASSIGNING <fs_delivery_item> WHERE lfimg > 0.
    SELECT SINGLE kosta INTO kostk FROM vbup WHERE kosta <> 'C' AND vbeln = <fs_delivery_item>-vbeln AND posnr = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.
    vbpok_ls-vbeln_vl = <fs_delivery_item>-vbeln.
    vbpok_ls-posnr_vl = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    vbpok_ls-vbeln = sy-datum.
    vbpok_ls-posnn = <fs_delivery_item>-posnr.
    vbpok_ls-lianp    = 'X'.
    vbpok_ls-lfimg    = <fs_delivery_item>-lfimg.
    vbpok_ls-taqui  = 'X'.
    vbpok_ls-pikmg = vbpok_ls-lfimg.
    APPEND vbpok_ls TO vbpok_lt.

    vbkok_ls-vbeln_vl = vbeln.
  ENDLOOP.

  CALL FUNCTION 'WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE'
    EXPORTING
      vbkok_wa                    = vbkok_ls
      COMMIT                      = 'X'
      if_error_messages_send_0    = 'X'
      update_picking              = 'X'
      nicht_sperren               = 'X'
      delivery                    = vbeln
    IMPORTING
      ef_error_any_0              = ef_error_any_0
      ef_error_in_item_deletion_0 = ef_error_in_item_deletion_0
      ef_error_in_pod_update_0    = ef_error_in_pod_update_0
      ef_error_in_interface_0     = ef_error_in_interface_0
      ef_error_in_goods_issue_0   = ef_error_in_goods_issue_0
      ef_error_in_final_check_0   = ef_error_in_final_check_0
      ef_error_partner_update     = ef_error_partner_update
      ef_error_sernr_update       = ef_error_sernr_update
    TABLES
      vbpok_tab                   = vbpok_lt
      prot                        = prot_lt.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      WAIT   = 'X'
    IMPORTING
      RETURN = return2.

entry.title: Re: trying to understand a concept 'Extend'
entry.content:
Hello @KY101 

Vendor master data is maintained on several organizational levels. It has a central data e.g. address, data on company code level (all the FI related stuff) and data on purchasing organization level (info related to purchasing). 

If you want to use a vendor number for a specific purchasing organization level, you need to create the vendor data on the purchasing organization level or in other words extend the vendor to the purchasing organization level.

The same concept works for materials that are maintained on central level, plant level, storage location level, sales area level etc. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski
